description: Web app (apps/web): produce a concrete UI/UX audit document, then implement the high-priority fixes it identifies. This covers the user's ask to 'analyse the web app project and find ui/ux issues'. The audit is written to the task folder; the fixes are targeted, low-risk UI improvements (navigation/back-button consistency, duplicate-component cleanup coordination with FEAT-004, obvious inconsistencies). Independent of the staff-app features; should run after FEAT-004 so dark-theme findings are not duplicated.
steps: Write .agents/tasks/task-staff-ui-web-audit/web-ui-ux-audit.md: a grounded audit of apps/web UI/UX. For each finding include the file/location, the problem, user impact, severity (high/med/low), and the recommended fix. Base it on actual code, not generic advice. Known concrete leads to include and verify: (a) back-navigation coverage in components/header.tsx uses a hardcoded `innerPages` list that omits /offers (coupons/deals) and other inner routes, and uses router.push(parentPath) instead of router.back() (loses history/scroll); (b) three duplicate theme-toggle components (cross-reference FEAT-004); (c) admin & delivery routes render no shared header/back affordance on desktop; (d) any inconsistent spacing/typography token usage vs tailwind.config.ts scale.

Implement the back-navigation fix in components/header.tsx: extend inner-page/back-button coverage to the missing routes (at minimum /offers) so settings/rewards(loyalty)/coupon(offers) pages get a persistent header with a working back control on desktop and mobile. Prefer router.back() with a safe fallback to the parent path when there is no history. Keep existing behavior for pages already covered.

Apply the other high-severity, low-risk fixes surfaced by the audit that are pure UI/UX and do not change data/auth/API behavior (e.g. token/spacing consistency, obvious missing hover/focus states, duplicate component consolidation coordinated with FEAT-004). Defer anything risky or out-of-scope and list it in the audit's 'Deferred / follow-up' section instead of implementing.
Do NOT attempt to fix backend/realtime concerns (e.g. SSE delivery-alert listeners) or add features; if the audit surfaces them, record them as follow-ups only.
Run `npx tsc --noEmit`, `npm run lint`, and `npm test` in apps/web and fix any errors introduced.
acceptance_criteria: web-ui-ux-audit.md exists in the task folder with specific, file-referenced findings (each with location, problem, impact, severity, recommended fix) rather than generic advice.

components/header.tsx provides a working back/persistent-header affordance for settings, rewards (loyalty) and coupon (offers) pages on both desktop and mobile, using router.back() with a safe parent-path fallback.
High-severity, low-risk UI/UX fixes from the audit are implemented; risky/out-of-scope items are explicitly listed as deferred follow-ups.
`npx tsc --noEmit`, `npm run lint`, and `npm test` pass in apps/web with no new errors vs baseline.

findings.audit_doc: Wrote .agents/tasks/task-staff-ui-web-audit/web-ui-ux-audit.md with grounded findings (each has file/location, problem, user impact, severity, recommended fix). High: H1 back-nav loses history/scroll (router.push), H2 /offers missing back affordance, H3 header 'Help & Support' link missing dark: variant. Medium: M1 offers page off-scale slate-* colors, M2 dark-toggle dedup (cross-refs FEAT-004, not redone). Low: L1 brittle query-route active state, L2 LocationIndicator layout shift. Deferred/follow-up section lists delivery route having no shared header/layout (app/delivery has no layout.tsx; Header returns null for /delivery), SSE/realtime out of scope, pre-existing lint warnings + test failures, and broader slate->token migration.
findings.header_backnav_fix: components/header.tsx: added handleBack() that prefers router.back() when window.history.length > 1 and falls back to router.push(parent) for direct/fresh loads. Added /offers to the innerPages list so it now gets the persistent header + Back control on desktop and mobile (parentPath resolves to '/'). /account/settings and /account/loyalty (rewards) were already covered by the existing pathname.startsWith('/account/') clause; the real gap was /offers. IMPORTANT: handleBack (useCallback) was initially placed after the early `return null`, which eslint flagged as a rules-of-hooks error; moved it ABOVE the early return (computing the parent path inside the callback, deps [router, pathname]) to keep hook order stable. Both desktop and mobile back buttons now call handleBack.
findings.header_dark_fix: components/header.tsx: the desktop 'Help & Support' link used text-neutral-600 hover:text-neutral-900 with no dark variant (near-black on the dark:bg-neutral-950 header). Added dark:text-neutral-300 dark:hover:text-white to match sibling controls.
findings.offers_token_fix: app/offers/page.tsx: converted off-scale slate-* colors to semantic tokens (text-slate-900 dark:text-white -> text-foreground for the h1/h2/h3 headings and empty-state heading; text-slate-700 dark:text-slate-300 -> text-foreground for promo discount; text-slate-200 dark:text-slate-700 icon -> text-muted-foreground/40). Equivalent in light mode, dark values now come from the token layer. slate is not part of tailwind.config.ts (which defines a neutral scale + semantic tokens). Broader slate->token sweep deferred.
findings.not_touched: Did NOT touch globals.css (so scroll-preservation test #54 is unaffected), providers/auth/API, realtime/SSE, or apps/mobile-staff. No new dependencies. React untouched.
findings.verification: From apps/web: `npx prisma generate` then `npx tsc --noEmit` = 0 errors. `npm run lint` = 0 errors, 3 pre-existing warnings (no-img-element in customer-orders-client, exhaustive-deps in product-grid + use-order-tracking-socket) matching baseline. `npm test` = 61 pass / 2 fail; the 2 failures are exactly the pre-existing baseline ones (#47 order-checkout DELIVERY_OUT_OF_RANGE, #54 scroll-preservation touch-action). No new failures.
findings.artifacts: Reverted apps/web/tsconfig.tsbuildinfo (regenerated by tsc, tracked build cache) to keep the commit clean, matching FEAT-001/FEAT-004.
